description_long: This house features a rectangular main structure with a prominent gable roof constructed from light-colored wooden rafters, partially open to reveal the internal framework. The exterior walls are made of gray masonry blocks, forming both the main building and an attached L-shaped extension that appears to be an unfinished or partially enclosed courtyard or garage area. The roof includes a central chimney and overhangs on both sides, with visible trusses and no apparent roofing material yet installed. There are no visible windows or doors, suggesting the structure is under construction or in a schematic phase. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on structural elements rather than decorative features.
description: A rectangular house with a gable roof, exposed wooden rafters, and gray masonry walls, featuring an attached L-shaped extension and no visible windows or doors.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the gable roof and the attached L-shaped extension.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the exposed wooden rafters and the open interior layout.
description_view_2: Opposite side view of the house, mirroring the gable roof and extension seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the open courtyard or garage area and the back of the main structure.
attribute_entryway_design: main entryway not visible; structure appears unfinished with no defined door or porch
